healthspan.definition: The period of life spent free from chronic disease, disability, and functional decline.
healthspan.vulgarized_analogy: The portion of a vehicle's life where it runs smoothly without engine trouble, rather than just the total years before it goes to the scrap yard.

citations.text: Gems D. What is healthspan? and how can we measure it? Biogerontology. 2015;16(3):289-297.
citations.defining_quote: Healthspan is the period of life that is free from serious disease and chronic disability.

metabolic syndrome.definition: A cluster of metabolic dysregulations (insulin resistance, high blood pressure, abdominal obesity) that elevate cardiovascular disease risk.
metabolic syndrome.vulgarized_analogy: A series of small, concurrent electrical faults in a building's wiring that together threaten to trigger a major power failure.

citations.text: Alberti KG, et al. Harmonizing the metabolic syndrome: a joint interim statement. Circulation. 2009;120(16):1640-1645.
citations.defining_quote: The metabolic syndrome is a cluster of the most dangerous heart attack risk factors: diabetes and prediabetes, abdominal obesity, high cholesterol and high blood pressure.

cardiorespiratory fitness.definition: The capacity of the respiratory and circulatory systems to supply oxygen to working muscles during sustained exertion (often measured via VO2 max).
cardiorespiratory fitness.vulgarized_analogy: The volumetric throughput and pumping capacity of a water cooling system under full engine load.

citations.text: Raghuveer G, et al. Cardiorespiratory Fitness in Youth: An Important, Much Needed, but Overlooked Risk Factor. Circulation. 2020;142(7):e101-e118.
citations.defining_quote: Cardiorespiratory fitness (CRF) refers to the capacity of the circulatory and respiratory systems to supply oxygen to skeletal muscle mitochondria for energy production during physical activity.

GRADE.definition: Grading of Recommendations, Assessment, Development, and Evaluation; a standardized framework for rating the quality of scientific evidence from High to Very Low.
GRADE.vulgarized_analogy: An industry inspection framework that rates build quality from precision-certified down to unvetted home assembly.

citations.text: Guyatt GH, et al. GRADE: an emerging consensus on rating quality of evidence and strength of recommendations. BMJ. 2008;336(7650):924-926.
citations.defining_quote: The GRADE system classifies the quality of evidence into one of four grades—high, moderate, low, or very low.

Evidence Grade.definition: A standardized assessment based on the GRADE framework to rate the quality of research supporting an entry, ranging from High to Very Low.
Evidence Grade.vulgarized_analogy: A safety certification rating stamped on a structural beam based on the rigor of its stress tests.

citations.text: Balshem H, et al. GRADE guidelines: 3. Rating the quality of evidence. J Clin Epidemiol. 2011;64(4):401-406.
citations.defining_quote: The quality of evidence reflects the extent to which our confidence in an estimate of the effect is sufficient to support a particular recommendation.

Porphyromonas gingivalis.definition: A keystone bacterial pathogen in chronic periodontitis, linked to systemic inflammation and neurodegeneration.
Porphyromonas gingivalis.vulgarized_analogy: A rust-causing microbe that starts in the plumbing system but eventually migrates and degrades the central processor.

citations.text: Dominy SS, et al. Porphyromonas gingivalis in Alzheimer's disease brains: Evidence for disease causation and treatment with small-molecule inhibitors. Sci Adv. 2019;5(1):eaau3333.
citations.defining_quote: Porphyromonas gingivalis, the defining pathogen in chronic periodontitis, was identified in the brains of Alzheimer's disease patients.

gingipains.definition: A group of toxic protease enzymes secreted by Porphyromonas gingivalis, found in postmortem Alzheimer's brains.
gingipains.vulgarized_analogy: Corrosive chemical wastes released by an invasive pathogen that actively dissolve central circuit connections.

citations.text: Potempa J, et al. Gingipains, the major cysteine proteinases of Porphyromonas gingivalis. Biol Chem. 1997;378(3-4):193-200.
citations.defining_quote: Gingipains are cell-associated and secreted cysteine proteases of P. gingivalis that play a major role in virulence.

atuzaginstat.definition: An experimental small-molecule inhibitor of Porphyromonas gingivalis gingipains, tested for Alzheimer's treatment.
atuzaginstat.vulgarized_analogy: A targeted molecular cap designed to lock onto corrosive chemical outputs and neutralize them before they dissolve circuits.

citations.text: ClinicalTrials.gov study. GAIN Trial: Phase 2/3 study of atuzaginstat in mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's disease. NCT03828240.
citations.defining_quote: Atuzaginstat (COR388) is an orally administered, brain-penetrant, small-molecule inhibitor of gingipains.

xenohormesis.definition: The biological hypothesis that organisms have evolved to respond to stress-induced chemical signals in plants.
xenohormesis.vulgarized_analogy: An early warning radar system where a device reads distress signals emitted by neighboring units to prepare for incoming storms.

citations.text: Howitz KT, Sinclair DA. Xenohormesis: sensing the chemical cues of other species' adversity. Cell. 2008;133(3):387-391.
citations.defining_quote: Xenohormesis is the hypothesis that organisms have evolved to respond to stress-induced chemical cues from other species in their environment.

phytochemicals.definition: Biologically active compounds produced by plants, often acting as molecular defense and signaling keys.
phytochemicals.vulgarized_analogy: Specialized defense chemicals synthesized by a factory to ward off intruders, which external systems utilize for tune-ups.

citations.text: Gibson EL, et al. Phytochemicals: Nutritional significance of fruit and vegetables. Nutrition Bulletin. 1998;23(2):121-131.
citations.defining_quote: Phytochemicals are defined as non-nutritive plant chemicals that have protective or disease preventive properties.

sulforaphane.definition: An organosulfur compound found in cruciferous vegetables that potently activates the Nrf2 defense pathway.
sulforaphane.vulgarized_analogy: A chemical trigger that simulates a minor threat, forcing the cellular defense system to boot up and run clean-up tasks.

citations.text: Fahey JW, et al. Broccoli sprouts: an exceptionally rich source of inducers of enzymes that protect against chemical carcinogens. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A. 1997;94(19):10367-10372.
citations.defining_quote: Sulforaphane is an isothiocyanate that is a highly potent inducer of phase 2 detoxification enzymes.

EGCG.definition: Epigallocatechin gallate; a major polyphenol in green tea that modulates AMPK signaling and metabolic pathways.
EGCG.vulgarized_analogy: A molecular bypass switch that plugs into metabolic control units to stimulate energy efficiency routines.

citations.text: Singh BN, et al. Green tea catechin, epigallocatechin-3-gallate (EGCG): mechanisms, perspectives and clinical applications. Arch Biochem Biophys. 2011;508(1):1-8.
citations.defining_quote: Epigallocatechin-3-gallate (EGCG) is the most abundant and biologically active polyphenol in green tea.

resveratrol.definition: A natural polyphenol found in red grapes and berries that acts as an activator of the sirtuin pathway.
resveratrol.vulgarized_analogy: An auxiliary throttle that interfaces with sirtuin controllers to accelerate DNA repair and resource-conservation routines.

citations.text: Baur JA, Sinclair DA. Therapeutic potential of resveratrol: the in vivo evidence. Nat Rev Drug Discov. 2006;5(6):493-506.
citations.defining_quote: Resveratrol (3,5,4'-trihydroxystilbene) is a polyphenol that has been shown to extend lifespan in diverse organisms.

quercetin.definition: A plant flavonoid and senolytic compound found in onions, apples, and berries.
quercetin.vulgarized_analogy: A specialized cleaning agent that helps scrub away worn-out cellular machinery that refuse to self-terminate.

citations.text: Li Y, et al. Quercetin, Inflammation and Immunity. Nutrients. 2016;8(3):167.
citations.defining_quote: Quercetin is a natural flavonoid widely present in fruits and vegetables, possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities.

fisetin.definition: A dietary senolytic flavonoid shown to reduce senescent cell burden and extend healthspan in pre-clinical models.
fisetin.vulgarized_analogy: A targeted cleanup script that scans for hung, unresponsive cellular processes and safely kills them to free resources.

citations.text: Yousefzadeh MJ, et al. Fisetin is a senotherapeutic that extends healthspan and lifespan. EBioMedicine. 2018;36:109-122.
citations.defining_quote: Fisetin is a natural flavonoid with potent senolytic activity in mice and human tissues.

curcumin.definition: The primary bioactive diarylheptanoid in turmeric, known for modulating NF-kB inflammatory signaling pathways.
curcumin.vulgarized_analogy: A chemical buffer that blocks the core trigger of system-wide alarm signals, keeping baseline thermal levels stable.

citations.text: Aggarwal BB, et al. Curcumin: the Indian solid gold. Adv Exp Med Biol. 2007;595:1-75.
citations.defining_quote: Curcumin (diferuloylmethane) is the active component of the golden spice turmeric, which modulates multiple signaling pathways.

Nrf2.definition: A key transcription factor that regulates the expression of antioxidant proteins protecting against oxidative damage.
Nrf2.vulgarized_analogy: The master emergency response coordinator that initiates system-wide maintenance and defense protocols.

citations.text: Ma Q. Role of Nrf2 in Oxidative Stress and Toxicity. Annu Rev Pharmacol Toxicol. 2013;53:401-426.
citations.defining_quote: Nrf2 is a basic leucine zipper transcription factor that coordinates the expression of cytoprotective genes.

SIRT1.definition: Sirtuin 1; a NAD+-dependent deacetylase regulating chromatin structure, DNA repair, and cellular maintenance.
SIRT1.vulgarized_analogy: A quality-control manager that walks the production line, repairing damaged instructions and shutting down inefficient modules.

citations.text: Carafa V, et al. Sirtuin functions and modulation: from biology to clinical applications. Clin Epigenetics. 2016;8:61.
citations.defining_quote: Sirtuin 1 (SIRT1) is a nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD+)-dependent deacetylase that plays key roles in longevity.

NF-kB.definition: Nuclear factor kappa-light-chain-enhancer of activated B cells; a protein complex controlling cytokine production.
NF-kB.vulgarized_analogy: A hair-trigger fire alarm switch that, when bumped, sets off loud alarms and sprinkler systems throughout the facility.

citations.text: Gilmore TD. Introduction to NF-kB: a participant in many biological pathways. Oncogene. 2006;25(51):6680-6684.
citations.defining_quote: NF-kB is a family of transcription factors that play critical roles in inflammation, immunity, and cell survival.

senolytics.definition: Agents or compounds designed to selectively induce death in senescent cells to halt tissue degradation.
senolytics.vulgarized_analogy: Precision demolition charges set up to collapse decrepit, structurally unsafe buildings that are blocking new construction.

citations.text: Kirkland JL, Tchkonia T. Senolytic agents: From discovery to translation. J Intern Med. 2020;288(5):518-536.
citations.defining_quote: Senolytics are drugs that selectively eliminate senescent cells by temporarily disabling the networks that protect them.

SAM cycle.definition: S-adenosylmethionine cycle; a universal metabolic pathway providing methyl groups for epigenetic DNA modification.
SAM cycle.vulgarized_analogy: A recycling conveyor belt that continuously refurbishes molecular stamps used to index and lock down files in database libraries.

citations.text: Ouyang Y, et al. S-Adenosylmethionine Cycle Adaptations in Stress and Disease. Int J Mol Sci. 2020;21(21):7895.
citations.defining_quote: The S-adenosylmethionine (SAM) cycle regulates cellular methylation reactions and is vital for biochemical homeostasis.
